title: James Earl Jones
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Otheruses|James|more=Jones}} {{Person |image=[[File:JamesEarlJones.png]] |name=James Earl Jones |birth=[[January 17]], [[1930s#1931|1931]]
[[Wikipedia:Mississippi|Mississippi]]{{SWArchive|url=bio/jamesearljones.html|text=James Earl Jones}} |death=[[September 9]], [[2024]]
[[Wikipedia:New York|New York]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.rollingstone.com/tv-movies/tv-movie-news/james-earl-jones-dead-obituary-1234768096|text=James Earl Jones, Voice of Darth Vader, 'Lion King,' Dead at 93|author=Daniel Kreps|date=2024-09-09|work=[[Wikipedia:Rolling Stone|''Rolling Stone'']]|archivedate=20240910023129}} |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] |occupation=*Film actor *Voice actor |companies= |website= }} {{Quote|The rumor is that he thought of Orson Welles, and then probably thought that Orson might be too recognizable, so what he ends up picking is a voice that was born in Mississippi, raised in Michigan, and was a stutterer. And, that happened to be my voice.|James Earl Jones, on how George Lucas chose him to voice Darth Vader|{{SW|url=news/james-earl-jones|text=James Earl Jones: 1931-2024}}}} '''James Earl Jones''' ([[January 17]], [[1930s#1931|1931]] – [[September 9]], [[2024]]) was an American actor who voiced [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] in the [[Star Wars saga|''Star Wars'' saga]]. A renowned stage actor, he dubbed over [[David Prowse]]'s performance in Vader's [[Darth Vader's armor|suit]] for the [[original trilogy]], with [[Bob Anderson]] also serving as Vader's stuntman and [[Sebastian Shaw]] playing the unmasked Vader. Jones also performed some of Vader's dialogue in ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' of [[1978]], the animated television series ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' between [[2014]] and [[2016]], and the latter year's ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]''. He also voiced additional lines in [[2005]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']] and [[2019]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''The Rise of Skywalker'']]. Jones lended his voice as Darth Vader for [[Lucasfilm]] to use via an artificial intelligence program for the [[2022]] television series ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', with [[Hayden Christensen]] performing as Vader in his suit. ==Biography== ===Early life and career=== {{Quote|They taught us not to put ethnic or gender limits on the characters that we studied. They taught us speech for Shakespeare, and speech for Arthur Miller, and speech for Tennessee Williams, and speech for George Lucas.|James Earl Jones|}} Born on [[January 17]], [[1930s#1931|1931]] in [[Wikipedia:Mississippi|Mississippi]] state of the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States of America]], James Earl Jones became a stage actor despite growing up with a stutter. He was raised in [[Wikipedia:Michigan|Michigan]], his family having moved north to that state along with other [[Wikipedia:African Americans|African Americans]] as part of the [[Wikipedia:Great Migration (African American)|Great Migration]], and Jones served in the [[Wikipedia:United States Army|US Army]] within the country during the [[Wikipedia:Korean War|Korean War]]. He then moved to [[Wikipedia:New York City|New York City]] to pursue a career in drama at the [[Wikipedia:American Theatre Wing|American Theatre Wing]]. Jones became known as a distinguished actor of [[Wikipedia:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare's]] plays on [[Wikipedia:Broadway theatre|Broadway]]. He debuted on film with a small role in director [[Wikipedia:Stanley Kubrick|Stanley Kubrick's]] 1964 [[Wikipedia:Dr. Strangelove|''Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb'']] and featured in test films for the children's television series [[Wikipedia:Sesame Street|''Sesame Street'']] in 1969. Jones additionally won a [[Wikipedia:Tony Awards|Tony Award]] for his role in the [[Wikipedia:Howard Sackler|Howard Sackler]] play [[Wikipedia:The Great White Hope|''The Great White Hope'']] in 1969, was nominated for an [[Wikipedia:Academy Awards|Academy Award]] for his role in the 1970 [[Wikipedia:The Great White Hope (film)|film adaptation]], and co-starred alongside [[Wikipedia:Diahann Carroll|Diahann Carroll]] in the 1974 film [[Wikipedia:Claudine (film)|''Claudine'']]. Jones also played [[Wikipedia:Malcolm X|Malcolm X]] in the 1977 [[Wikipedia:Muhammad Ali|Muhammad Ali]] film [[Wikipedia:The Greatest (1977 film)|''The Greatest'']], won another Tony Award for his part in the [[Wikipedia:August Wilson|August Wilson]] play [[Wikipedia:Fences (play)|''Fences'']] in [[1987]], and voiced Mufasa in Disney's [[1994]] [[Wikipedia:The Lion King|''The Lion King'']]. ===The voice of Vader=== {{Quote|David Prowse worked very hard to create Darth Vader…I take no credit for the character. I think that performance is in your head. Vader is good proof that if you stay out of the way of a character…good, effective dialogue will evoke in the viewer or listener all kinds of wonderful things that come from the viewer's mind.|James Earl Jones|}} [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] was played by [[David Prowse]], encased in an [[Darth Vader's armor|armored suit]].''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes IV–VI, 1977–1983]]'' While [[George Lucas]] initially considered asking filmmaker [[Wikipedia:Orson Welles|Orson Welles]] with his famous deep voice to voice Darth Vader, Lucas decided to cast James Earl Jones, who "won hands down" as he "created, with very little dialogue, one of the greatest villains that ever lived." In [[March]] of [[1977]], James Earl Jones spent only one day recording Vader's voice-over dialogue for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] at [[Wikipedia:Samuel Goldwyn Studio|Goldwyn Studios]].[[Star Wars Insider 95|''Star Wars Insider'' 95]] As such, Jones refused to be credited on ''Star Wars'' and [[1980]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], even going as far as telling people he did not do the voice-over. He recalled that upon reading Vader's line "''No, ''I'' am your father''" for ''The Empire Strikes Back'', he believed that Vader was lying and wondered how the events of ''Star Wars'' would unfold from there. While Prowse continued his role as Vader, he was also joined by the swordmaster [[Bob Anderson]] in the lightsaber duels of ''Empire'' and the next film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. With Prowse and Anderson reprising their roles in Vader's suit and Jones returning to voice Vader for ''Return of the Jedi'', Jones finally allowed himself to be credited as Vader's voice for the film's release in [[1983]].{{WebCite|url=http://usatoday30.usatoday.com/life/movies/news/2009-01-22-james-earl-jones_N.htm|text=A lifetime of achievement, but James Earl Jones isn't done|author=Susan Wloszczyna|date=2009-01-25|work=[[Wikipedia:USA Today|''USA Today'']]|archivedate=20160410153816}} Veteran actor [[Sebastian Shaw]] additionally performed as the unmasked Vader, having been redeemed by the character's son, [[Luke Skywalker]], played by [[Mark Hamill]]. Playing the newly introduced [[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian]] in ''The Empire Strikes Back'', [[Billy Dee Williams]] told [[Wikipedia:Rolling Stone|''Rolling Stone'']] magazine in 1980 that he had spoken with George Lucas about the racialist implications of contrasting the evil Darth Vader, "the dark, ''black'' fear," with the "''white'' knight" of [[Alec Guinness]] as [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. Williams, himself a black actor, then said that the addition of Lando Calrissian—a black character who is ultimately on the side of the protagonists—makes clear that ''Star Wars'' is instead focusing on symbolism divorced from skin color, with "darkness" being averse to "clarity." With audiences also learning that Darth Vader was a [[human]] who had been corrupted by the [[dark side of the Force]], Williams thought that the man, rather than just a "mechanical monster," has become a more frightening character.{{WebCite|url=https://www.rollingstone.com/culture/culture-news/star-wars-slaves-to-the-empire-61931|text='Star Wars': Slaves to the 'Empire'|author=Timothy White|work=[[Wikipedia:Rolling Stone|''Rolling Stone'']]|archivedate=20190519150233}} James Earl Jones himself stated that his job "was to keep awareness out of Darth Vader's voice—take all the humanity out of it." Specifically, he kept the voice within strict inflection boundaries, where "there was no way to enlighten the voice with awareness." After attending a comic-book convention, Jones said that he was "happy to be a part of the whole cult movement" of ''Star Wars'' even though some fans were "fanatics." ===Further ''Star Wars'' work=== {{Quote|James was an incredible actor, a most unique voice both in art and spirit. For nearly half a century he was Darth Vader, but the secret to it all is he was a beautiful human being. He gave depth, sincerity and meaning to all his roles, amongst the most important being devoted husband to the late Ceci and dad to Flynn. James will be missed by so many of us…friends and fans alike.|George Lucas|}} [[File:JamesEarlJones.jpg|thumb|left|270px|James Earl Jones in 2009]] By [[2005]], James Earl Jones returned to the role of Vader for George Lucas' [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. [[Hayden Christensen]] played as Anakin Skywalker and, once the character has fallen to the dark side of [[the Force]], as the suited Darth Vader. While the film was in production, Jones spoke of Christensen: "I see great potential toward all the Darth Vader choices in that young man." Of the character, Jones later said that "Darth Vader doesn't think he's evil," and the addition of the [[prequel trilogy]] meant that the ''Star Wars'' story—which Lucas himself described as the "tragedy" of Anakin Skywalker''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes I–III, 1999–2005]]''—has become: "How does one become evil and how is one redeemed from it?" In [[2011]], James Earl Jones received an [[Wikipedia:Academy Honorary Award|honorary]] Academy Award, completing his [[Wikipedia:List of EGOT winners|EGOT]] roster.{{Twitter|VP|status/1833589507305992699|[[Wikipedia:Vice President of the United States|Vice President]] [[Wikipedia:Kamala Harris|Kamala Harris]]|date=2024-09-10|archivedate=20240911061821}} Jones reprised his role as Darth Vader's voice in several episodes of the television series ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' and in the [[2016]] [[Star Wars Anthology Series|anthology]] film ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]''.{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/article/2016/06/23/darth-vader-rogue-one-star-wars|text=Darth Vader Lives!|author=Anthony Breznican|date=2016-06-23|work=''[[Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20200108111140}} In the [[Disney+]] series ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', Vader's voice was artificially created with [[Respeecher]], which uses archival recordings and a proprietary artificial intelligence algorithm to create new dialogue with the voices of performers. Respeecher, which also created the voice of young Luke Skywalker for ''[[Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett]]'', was used as Jones had stepped back from the role. After recording a short line for [[2019]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']], Jones had mentioned he was looking into winding down Vader to [[Matthew Wood]] at [[Skywalker Sound]]. Wood presented Jones with Respeecher's work, and the actor signed off on using his archival voice recordings to keep Vader alive and vital even by artificial means. Jones is credited for guiding the performance on ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'', and Wood described his contribution as "a benevolent godfather." They informed the actor about their plans for Vader and heeded his advice on how to stay on the right course. As a [[Wikipedia:Ukraine|Ukrainian]] start-up with employees in [[Wikipedia:Kyiv|Kyiv]] and [[Wikipedia:Lviv|Lviv]], Respeecher communicated extensively with Matthew Wood and director [[Deborah Chow]] on creating Darth Vader's dialogue in the days leading up to the [[Wikipedia:Russian invasion of Ukraine|Russian invasion of Ukraine]] on [[February 24]], 2022, and continued fine-tuning Vader's voice amidst Russian bombardments.{{WebCite|url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2022/09/darth-vaders-voice-emanated-from-war-torn-ukraine|text=Darth Vader's Voice Emanated From War-Torn Ukraine|author=Anthony Breznican|date=2022-09-23|work=[[Wikipedia:Vanity Fair (magazine)|''Vanity Fair'']]|archivedate=20220923151721}} While James Earl Jones reflected that he was very pleased with being "a part of that whole legend" of ''Star Wars'', he said that he was "just an observer. I'm a special effect, you know, at best." ===Death and legacy=== James Earl Jones passed away on [[September 9]], [[2024]], with a plethora of tributes. US [[Wikipedia:Vice President of the United States|Vice President]] [[Wikipedia:Kamala Harris|Kamala Harris]] praised Jones for overcoming his stutter and "every obstacle to dazzle the world on stage and screen," voicing "some of the most notable characters in our culture" and challenging "America's thinking on civil rights and race." Lucasfilm's President [[Kathleen Kennedy]] described Jones as "one of the most versatile and talented actors of our time," applauding his "commanding presence on screen" and "warm personality off screen." George Lucas lauded Jones' sincerity to his roles and devotion as husband to [[Wikipedia:Cecilia Hart|Cecilia Hart]] and father to Flynn Jones. [[C-3PO]] actor [[Anthony Daniels]] applauded the late Jones's acting talents and compassion in a social media post,{{Twitter|ADaniels3PO|status/1833395657463210090|[[Anthony Daniels]]|quote=James Earl Jones was the sweetest man who played the badest villain. Now, that's acting. His voice will be with us, always.|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/ZK4Nz}} while Hamill put out a post of his own on the passing of his on-screen father.{{Twitter|MarkHamill|status/1833246565168803985|[[Mark Hamill]]|quote=#RIP dad 💔|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/Qoa0S}} ==Works== ===Filmography=== {| {{Prettytable}} !
